title: Brainstorm: First Settlement
description: Sling idea agents to brainstorm the first settlement in this new world. What is this place, what do people do here, what makes it interesting?
closeReason: Brainstormed first settlement: Haul's Rest, a river-ford waystation turned permanent town. Local lens pitch sent. Haulers' Guild vs. settler conflict, mundane economy (farrier/bonesetter/harness repair), no cliches.

title: Create Caltworks settlement file
description: Write vault/Compendium/World Atlas/Settlements/Caltworks.md. Salt-rendering town that grew from seasonal camp. Calt family controls evaporation flats by squatter's right. Newer arrivals resent salt tithe. Most powerful person: woman who decides whose salt gets weighed first. White-crusted streets, brine/woodsmoke smell. No walls, no defensible position.
closeReason: Created Caltworks.md: salt-rendering settlement with Calt family tithe conflict, Harla Calt as weighing yard gatekeeper, degrading flats as hidden GM pressure.

title: Create Caltworks NPCs and registry
description: Write 2-3 NPC files in vault/Compendium/NPCs/ and update vault/NPCRegistry.md. Key NPCs: (1) The salt weigher, the de facto most powerful person in town. (2) A Calt family head who claims ownership of the flats. (3) A newcomer hauler who represents the resentment. Ground these in the salt economy.
closeReason: Created Maren Seedeck, Oran Calt, Dessa Vael NPC files and updated NPCRegistry.md. Three NPCs grounded in Caltworks salt economy with distinct motivations and interconnected tensions.

title: Fix NPC references in Caltworks.md
description: Replace Harla Calt references with Maren Seedeck, replace Pem Varsk references with Dessa Vael, and add Oran Calt as notable resident. The worldwriter and charwriter used different names; the charwriter names are canonical.
closeReason: Fixed NPC references in Caltworks.md: Harla Calt -> Maren Seedeck, Pem Varsk -> Dessa Vael, added Oran Calt. Updated GM secrets to match canonical charwriter files.

title: Update CHANGELOG, Timeline, and World Overview
description: Update vault/CHANGELOG.md with what was built (Caltworks settlement, 3 NPCs). Update vault/Timeline.md with Caltworks founding events. Update vault/World Overview.md with emerging themes: salt economy, labor tension, frontier settlement growing beyond its informal governance.
closeReason: Updated CHANGELOG, Timeline, and World Overview with Caltworks founding, 3 NPCs, timeline from founding to present, and emerging world themes.

title: Brainstorm: Salt buyer settlement
description: Who buys the salt from Caltworks? What settlement is at the other end of the road? What do they produce that Caltworks needs?
closeReason: Pitched Harrow Fell: a tannery town that buys salt for hide-curing, sends timber back west as return cargo, creating a mutual dependency with Caltworks neither settlement discusses openly.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (pragmatist)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? Where does it go? What does the trade route look like? Use the PRAGMATIST lens.
closeReason: Pragmatist brainstorm complete: identified fishing settlements as dominant buyer, 3-leg cart-based trade route, merchant factors as price-extracting middlemen, and the tiered weigh fee / hauler stoppage offers as buyer-vs-buyer competition using Caltworks tensions as leverage. Shrinking flats represent undisclosed supply risk to buyers.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (weird)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? What unexpected angle exists? Use the WEIRD lens.
closeReason: Brainstorm complete. 5 weird-lens ideas generated for 'who buys Caltworks salt': (1) buyer wants mineral byproduct not salt, (2) buyer is a land acquisition scout exploiting known flat degradation, (3) buyer is someone workers would refuse to supply, (4) shipment volume fraud in the weighing yard, (5) generational tithe obligation to an unknown recipient. Best gameplay angles: land acquisition (ticking clock antagonist), wrong commodity (investigation hook), unconscionable buyer (moral conflict). Results mailed to coordinator.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (local)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? What do locals think about the buyers? Use the LOCAL lens.
closeReason: Brainstormed who buys Caltworks salt from the local perspective. Identified merchant factors as primary visible buyer type, worker attitudes toward buyers, tensions around the Dessa factor offer and Calt exclusive buyer relationships, and what locals don't know about buyer-side competition and flat degradation.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (player)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? What makes this gameable? Use the PLAYER lens.
closeReason: Brainstormed 7 salt buyer types from player lens: smoker/curer, wholesale factor, garrison quartermaster, estate steward, road merchant, competing supplier's agent, and tanner. Identified cross-cutting player dynamics around information asymmetry (degrading flats), timing plays (factor stoppage scheme), and buyer choices as implicit faction support. Anti-cliche check applied throughout.

title: Lore check: salt trade direction
description: Verify that establishing a fishing settlement as primary salt buyer, a waypoint staging yard, and a land-acquisition merchant factor does not contradict existing vault content or hit anti-cliche rules.
closeReason: Lore check complete. All three proposed elements (fishing settlement as salt buyer, waypoint staging yard, land-acquisition merchant factor) are clear of contradictions and anti-cliche violations. One coherence note: clarify whether the land-acquisition factor is the same unnamed merchant factor already in vault (Caltworks.md:74, Dessa Vael.md:53) or a new distinct actor.

title: Create fishing settlement (salt buyer)
description: Create the fishing/curing settlement that is Caltworks' primary salt buyer. See dispatch mail for full details.
closeReason: Created Rackby settlement file: fishing/curing town on Greymere lake, Caltworks' primary salt buyer via merchant factor Vess Cordham. Full format match with Caltworks. New NPCs named for charwriter follow-up: Vess Cordham, Tamma Wreich, Burl.

title: Create waypoint staging yard
description: Create the waypoint/staging yard between Caltworks and the fishing settlement. See dispatch mail for full details.
closeReason: Created The Fork waypoint staging yard between Caltworks and the fishing coast. File: vault/Compendium/World Atlas/Settlements/The Fork.md. Updated NPCRegistry and CHANGELOG.

title: Create merchant factor NPC
description: Create the merchant factor NPC who is behind the land acquisition play. See dispatch mail for full details.
closeReason: Created Vess Cordham NPC file and added registry entry. Mulch records updated.

title: Update cross-references: salt trade integration
description: Update existing vault files to integrate the new salt trade content (Rackby, The Fork, Vess Cordham). Update Dessa Vael, Maren Seedeck, Caltworks, Timeline, World Overview, CHANGELOG.
closeReason: Cross-reference integration complete: 6 vault files updated to connect Rackby, Vess Cordham, and The Fork to existing Caltworks/NPC content. Timeline, World Overview, and CHANGELOG updated.

title: Brainstorm: Cordham's western salt supplier (pragmatist)
description: Idea agent with pragmatist lens: Who is the secondary salt supplier Cordham has been negotiating with to the west? What is their settlement, what do they produce, and why would they deal with Cordham?
closeReason: Pitched Salter's Notch: ~80-person seasonal salt-scraping settlement 2 days west, coarse product, Cordham uses them for quality-blending to mask Caltworks degradation, deepens information asymmetry theme.

title: Brainstorm: Cordham's western salt supplier (weird)
description: Idea agent with weird lens: Who is the secondary salt supplier Cordham has been negotiating with to the west? What is their settlement, what do they produce, and why would they deal with Cordham?
closeReason: Pitched Silthen: ~80-person reed-cutting community on a silted estuary whose reed-burning yields unwanted grey brine salt. Cordham buys it cheap as disposal, blends it into degrading Caltworks output to mask quality decline. Three-way information asymmetry: Silthen doesn't know what it's for, Caltworks doesn't know about blending, Rackby doesn't know quality is drifting.

title: Brainstorm: Cordham's western salt supplier (local)
description: Idea agent with local lens: Who is the secondary salt supplier Cordham has been negotiating with to the west? What is their settlement, what do they produce, and why would they deal with Cordham?
closeReason: Pitched Drevock: 60-person seasonal pan-salt settlement 3 days west. Coarser/cheaper salt, no prior buyer. Cordham approached them quietly with exclusivity. They don't know they're leverage against Caltworks. Fits information asymmetry theme.

title: Brainstorm: Cordham's western salt supplier (player)
description: Idea agent with player lens: Who is the secondary salt supplier Cordham has been negotiating with to the west? What is their settlement, what do they produce, and why would they deal with Cordham?
closeReason: Pitched Kettleham: brine-spring hamlet run by the Harrock family, coarser gray salt, negotiating with Cordham for a volume contract. Player hook: PCs escort the factor west and discover this is why Cordham hasn't pressed Caltworks on salt quality.

title: Lore check: Silthen western supplier concept
description: Verify the Silthen concept against existing vault lore and anti-cliche rules. Silthen is an estuary reed-cutting settlement ~3-4 days west of Caltworks. They produce crude grey salt as a byproduct of burning brine-saturated reed beds. Cordham is buying it cheap and plans to blend it with declining Caltworks salt. Check for contradictions with existing geography, timeline, trade relationships, and tone.
closeReason: Silthen concept is lore-consistent. Fills pre-seeded 'secondary supplier to the west' slot in Cordham.md/Rackby.md/Timeline.md. Two items flagged: (1) clarify blend vs. transition strategy in Cordham's plan; (2) establish grey salt's fish-curing adequacy. No contradictions found. Geography gap (western estuary) is undefined but not conflicting.

title: Create Silthen settlement file
description: Worldwriter: Create vault/Compendium/World Atlas/Settlements/Silthen.md. Estuary reed-cutting settlement ~60-80 people, 3-4 days west of Caltworks. Key NPC: Hava Denn (manages reed plots and seasonal burns).
closeReason: Created Silthen.md: estuary reed-cutting settlement serving as Cordham's secret secondary salt supplier. Hava Denn (plot manager) and Pell Voel (pan operator) established as key NPCs with competing interests around salt expansion.

title: Create Hava Denn NPC + update NPCRegistry
description: Characterwriter: Create vault/Compendium/NPCs/Hava Denn.md and add Silthen NPCs to NPCRegistry. Hava Denn manages the communal reed plots at Silthen. Cordham's contact for grey salt purchases.
closeReason: Created Hava Denn NPC (Silthen reed plot manager, Cordham's grey salt contact) and added her to NPCRegistry. Grey salt is byproduct of seasonal reed-burning on brine-saturated silt.

title: Cross-reference updates for Silthen integration
description: Worldwriter: Update Cordham.md, Timeline.md, World Overview.md, Caltworks.md, Rackby.md with Silthen references and blending subplot details.
closeReason: Silthen cross-references integrated: Cordham named Silthen/Hava Denn/Pell Voel across 5 files; Timeline updated with Cordham visit; World Overview themes expanded; NPCRegistry updated.

title: One-shot: The Spoiled Season (salt trade crisis)
description: Adventuresmith: Create a one-shot adventure centered on the Rackby spoilage crisis and Cordham's secret supplier scheme. PCs arrive at the trade corridor and get drawn into the investigation. Situation-based, not plot-based.
closeReason: One-shot adventure The Spoiled Season created: three-act situation-based adventure using full existing lore, five outcome paths, no new NPCs invented.

title: Brainstorm: Fork information dynamics (pragmatist)
description: Idea agent brainstorm with pragmatist lens
closeReason: Brainstorm complete: pitched Cedd Miralle discovering Cordham's northeast branch salt smuggling through a mundane conversation with Praven Holdt. Miralle records the anomaly in his own ledger — evidence that Cordham is running Silthen supply through The Fork's logging blind spot. Also sent coordinator a full analysis of Fork information dynamics as additional context.

title: Brainstorm: Fork information dynamics (local)
description: Idea agent brainstorm with local lens
closeReason: Brainstorm complete: local lens pitch on Fork information dynamics. Tallin Oss accumulating dangerous ledger knowledge as byproduct of mundane log-keeping; second copy hidden, no one to trust with it. Mirrors byproduct economy theme, stays human scale, generates natural PC hooks.

title: Brainstorm: Fork information dynamics (player)
description: Idea agent brainstorm with player lens
closeReason: Brainstorm complete: pitched Miralle-sends-letter as the concrete next Fork event. Cordham reads the courier mail, rides to The Fork, asks Oss to hold logs. Players arrive with Cordham already present, Miralle gone, Oss sitting on eight seasons of records he has been asked not to release for the first time.

title: Create NPC files: Tallin Oss and Cedd Miralle

closeReason: Created NPC files for Tallin Oss and Cedd Miralle; updated NPCRegistry with wikilinks

closeReason: Updated The Fork GM section with Oss log copy and Miralle approach details; added recent-weeks Timeline section covering Miralle completing comparison and Cordham's hold-logs message to Oss.

title: Update CHANGELOG: Fork information dynamics

closeReason: Added changelog entry for The Fork information dynamics: Miralle's settled approach and Oss's precautionary log copy

title: Brainstorm: Tamma Wreich and Rackby politics (pragmatist)
closeReason: Pragmatist brainstorm complete: Tamma Wreich's political leverage derives from capital reserves that the spoilage crisis is eroding. Her move is to force accountability with Cordham now, before her positional advantage narrows to nothing. Pitch sent to coordinator.

title: Brainstorm: Tamma Wreich and Rackby politics (local)
closeReason: Brainstorm complete: Tamma Wreich NPC depth, Rackby council dynamics, north shore context, and wikilink gap. No lore contradictions found. Result mailed to coordinator.

title: Brainstorm: Tamma Wreich and Rackby politics (player)
closeReason: Reviewed Tamma Wreich and Rackby politics through player lens. No lore contradictions found. Key issues: missing NPC file for Tamma Wreich, not in NPCRegistry, broken wikilink for Tallin Oss in The Spoiled Season. Gaps: no physical description, no personal stakes, unnamed boat owner council members limit political play. Brainstorm hooks sent to coordinator.

title: Lore audit: full vault consistency check
closeReason: Full vault audit complete. Found 3 definitive contradictions (Hava Denn tenure 9 vs 11 years; Cedd Miralle relocation 3 years vs 2 seasons in Timeline; Cordham-Hava Denn start 4 seasons vs 2 seasons in Timeline), 3 NPCRegistry gaps (Tamma Wreich, Burl, Kel Dusk), wikilink convention violations in The Fork/Rackby/Silthen Notable Residents tables, frontmatter missing type field on 3 meta files and no frontmatter on CHANGELOG, em dashes in CHANGELOG headings. Anti-cliche and tone checks clean.

title: Brainstorm: Calt succession crisis (local)
closeReason: Lore audit complete. No contradictions found in succession crisis material. Identified gaps (unnamed eldest son, undefined claim scope), anti-cliche risks, and key lore constraints (tithe unenforceability, salt degradation, Cordham's concealment) that the brainstorm must respect. Result mail sent to coordinator.

title: Brainstorm: Calt succession crisis (weird)
closeReason: Brainstorm complete. Weird pitch: Caltworks succession crisis already happened via institutional capture — Maren Seedeck took real power six years ago when she took the weighing yard. Oran's son is trying to sell the Calt name before the market realizes it is hollow. Crisis is the revelation, not the event. Grounded in existing NPC files and information-asymmetry theme.

title: Brainstorm: North Flat breakaway (pragmatist)
closeReason: Brainstormed North Flat breakaway from pragmatist lens: drainage infrastructure dependency, consignment sales through The Fork bypassing Cordham, internal tithe dispute splitting the three families. Sent detailed result mail and specific 3-idea reply to dispatch.

title: Brainstorm: North Flat breakaway (weird)
closeReason: Brainstorm complete: 3 weird angles for the North Flat breakaway. (1) Families are actively suppressing yield data to stay below Calt/buyer interest threshold. (2) Nael's info-share is actually an asset appraisal for her own exit plan. (3) The north flat grey salt is the valuable product, Cordham has been buying it cheap through an intermediary, the meeting was triggered by one family learning they were being undersold.

title: Brainstorm: North Flat breakaway (local)
closeReason: Brainstorm pitch delivered: north flat families are timing a direct approach to Tamma Wreich at Rackby, bypassing Cordham. Not rebellion, a business arrangement. Grounded in existing lore (no Calt documentation, family connections, Wreich's boat-owner position).

title: Brainstorm: North Flat breakaway (player)
closeReason: Brainstormed 3 player-lens ideas for North Flat breakaway: (1) families filing formal charter petition, creating courier dilemma; (2) third family's unknown mineral discovery fracturing the breakaway coalition; (3) north flat absorbing main-flat defectors and replicating the same governance tensions it fled. All three ideas use information asymmetry, institutional fragility, and Bevan's pending sale as time pressure.
